title: amphithalea pageae
parts: Amphithalea pageae has many medicinal parts, such as leaves, roots, and stems. The leaves of Amphithalea pageae contain medicinal constituents like flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been traditionally used for their anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The roots of the plant are rich in saponins and phenolic acids, which may have antioxidant and cardiovascular benefits. Additionally, the stems of Amphithalea pageae contain glycosides and terpenoids, which have been reported to possess anti-cancer and anti-inflammatory activities.
side_effects: Amphithalea pageae can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Ingesting large quantities of the plant can also lead to headaches, dizziness, and in severe cases, seizures and coma. Prolonged exposure to the plant's volatile oils may cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and respiratory problems. Furthermore, improper use of Amphithalea pageae may interact with other medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions.
